title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.
evidence_quote: Entering air wet-bulb temperature: 60 to 85°F (if air is not dehumidified in the application, select coils based on sensible heat transfer)

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.
evidence_quote: Evaporator refrigerant saturation temperature: 30 to 55°F at coil suction outlet (refrigerant vapor superheat at coil suction outlet is 6°F or higher)

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.
evidence_quote: For ethylene glycol solution: 1 to 6 fps, 0 to 90°F entering dry-bulb temperature, 60 to 80°F entering wet-bulb temperature, 10 to 60% aqueous glycol concentration by weight

title: Coil Sensible Heat Ratio
summary: Typical range for the air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Sensible to total heat ratio range

evidence_quote: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies in practice from about 0.6 to 1.0 (i.e., sensible heat is from 60 to 100% of the total, depending on the application).

title: Coil Selection Velocity
summary: Typical air face velocity range for selecting dehumidifying coils for comfort applications.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Dehumidifying coil air face velocity range

evidence_quote: Dehumidifying coils for comfort applications are frequently selected in the range of 400 to 500 fpm air face velocity.

title: HTW Control Valve Sizing
summary: Pressure difference requirement for control valve sizing in high-temperature water systems.
evidence_quote: The valve and equipment must be sized to absorb, in the control valve at full flow, not less than half the available pressure difference between supply and return mains where the equipment is served.

title: HTW Control Valve Type
summary: Recommended valve type and material for high-temperature water systems.
evidence_quote: An equal-percentage valve is typically used. Stainless steel trim is recommended, and all valve body materials and packing should be suitable for high temperatures and pressures.

title: HTW Control Valve Location
summary: Typical location for control valves in high-temperature water systems to reduce operating temperature and prevent erosion.
evidence_quote: Control valves are commonly located in the return lines from heat transfer units to reduce the valve operating temperature and to prevent plug erosion caused by high-temperature water flashing to steam at lower discharge pressure.

title: HTW Generator Safety Controls
summary: Required safety control actions for high-temperature water generators to prevent tube failure.
evidence_quote: HTW generators require a minimum water flow at all times to prevent tube failure. Means should be provided to measure the flow and to stop combustion if the flow falls below the minimum value recommended by the generator manufacturer.

title: HTW Burner Control
summary: Recommended combustion control strategy for high-temperature water systems based on capacity.
evidence_quote: The rapid response through the generator makes it necessary to modulate the combustion rate on all systems with a capacity of over a few million Btu/h.

title: Ceiling Plenum Pressure
summary: Potential issue with ceiling plenum applications in high-rise buildings due to stack effect.
evidence_quote: Some ceiling plenum applications with lay-in panels do not work well where the stack effect of a high-rise building or high-rise elevators creates a negative pressure.
